repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: >Correspondence, Manuscripts, Newspapers, Microfilm, Printed Materials, Publications.
did.abstract: Correspondence; scrapbooks; journals; clippings; addresses; translations; drafts, manuscripts, and articles; printed copies of works by or about Goldwin Smith

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Historian, journalist, professor at Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence; scrapbooks; journals; clippings; addresses; translations; drafts, manuscripts, and articles; printed copies of works by or about Goldwin Smith; obituaries and memorials; photographs; personal correspondence and a few family papers; and materials gathered by Smith's secretary Theodore Arnold Haultain for a series of volumes he planned to publish on the works of Goldwin Smith.

Included is correspondence of Lord Ashbourne, Charles F. Benjamin, S.H.J. Böhme, Henri Bourassa (Canadian M.P.), John Bright, James Bryce, Joseph Chamberlain, W. Bourke Cochran, Sir Charles Dilke, Lord Farrer, W.E. Gladstone, George M. Grant, M.E. Grant-Duff, F. Greenwood, Earl Grey, James J. Hill, Hon. H.G. Joly, James Laister, Lord Lansdowne, Sir John A. MacDonald, Herbert E. Millholen (city editor, NEW YORK EVENING POST), Lord Minto, Lord Morley, Horace Plunkett, Anna P. Pruyn, John M. Robertson, Lord Rosebery, Charles B. Spahr (Anti-Imperialist League), James Strachey (editor, SPECTATOR), Professor James Sully, Phillips Thompson, Professor Tyndall, General J.H. Wilson, Mrs. Emma Winkworth, and Viscount Wolseley.

Goldwin Smith's correspondence with members of the faculty and administration of Cornell University not only reveals his concern for that institution, but also relates to literary and political subjects. Included is correspondence of George Lincoln Burr, Hiram Corson, George W. Curtis, Willard Fiske, Jacob Gould Schurman, Moses Coit Tyler, and Andrew D. White. A series of letters written between 1868 and 1870 to George Waring in England records Smith's initial reactions to the new university and toward American places, people, and attitudes. The University of Toronto and Queens University in Kingston are the subjects of correspondence with J.W. Flavelle and George M. Grant.

A.H. Beesly, A.V. Dicey, C.H. Firth, E.A. Freeman, George Otto Trevelyan, and P. Villari are among the historians who corresponded with Goldwin Smith; their letters comment upon current social and political trends and events, as well as upon their work as historians. Among other correspondents are Charles Francis Adams, the Duke of Argyll, Matthew Arnold, W.J. Ashley, General Lord Bryce (NORTH AMERICAN REVIEW), Andrew Carnegie, Lord Chief Justice Coleridge, D.C. Gilman, General Sir Fred Middleton, Julian Pauncefote, Viscount Peel, and Carl Schurz.
There are also some family papers, including journals of Smith's mother, Elizabeth Breton Smith.
Collection also includes Goldwin Smith's academic robe from Oxford and hood from McMasters University.

English, Canadian, and American political issues (ca. 1880-1910) dominate the correspondence of Goldwin Smith: the possibility of a Canada-United States union, commercial or political; free trade vs. protection; party maneuvering in all three countries; the problem of the French Canadian element; the Irish Home Rule struggle; local and municipal government reform; imperialism, particularly as it was manifested in the Boer and Spanish-American Wars; the yellow press; the women's suffrage movement; the Jew in the modern national state; socialism and the increasing importance of labor groups in government; British policy toward Russia; the Canadian-Pacific Railroad and its influence on government; and related topics.

p.extref: PDF of item-level lists for boxes available.

otherfindaid.head: Other Finding Aids
otherfindaid.p: Link to the printed microfilm collection guide. PDF file. Contains additional biographical information.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Goldwin Smith. Papers, #14-17-134. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Grant Duff, Mountstuart E., Sir (Mountstuart Elphinstone), 1829-1906.
Lansdowne, Henry Charles Keith Petty-FitzMaurice, Marquess of, 1845-1927.

Rosebery, Archibald Philip Primrose, Earl of, 1847-1929.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Letters copied from other collections for the microfilm are interfiled

did.unittitle: Series II. Manuscripts, Printed Material, Clippings, and Biographical Information

otherfindaid.head: Other Finding Aids
otherfindaid.p: PDF of item-level lists for boxes 41-53 available via link at the top of this guide.

did.unittitle: Lists of pamphlets and "Weekly Sun" articles, of contributions to the "Canadian Monthly", MS fragments, and a list of correspondents for a proposed book of Smith letters prepared by TA Haultain
Index and bibliographical cards prepared by Haultain
MS of introduction and much of the text of "The United States", written in the hand of Charles F. Benjamin
Typescript of a projected volume of Smith letters "comprising chiefly letters to his American friends"
Partial scrapbook of clippings in England
Two loose-leaf typescript copies of a Hewett bibliography of Smith works
Carbon copy of the membership of the National Continental Union League
Galley proof of "My Memory of Gladstone", and others of the same work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "The Early Days of Cornell" typescript, "Abraham Lincoln" typescripts and clipping, "Chatham", "Cromwell", "Labour and Socialism", "Peace with Justice", "Guesses at the Riddle of Existence", and miscellaneous fragments

did.unittitle: MS of Goldwin Smith address at the laying of the cornerstone of Goldwin Smith Hall, bound in red leather
Letter from Smith to Schurman (1903) and accompanying letter from Dean of Arts and Sciences (1918)
Letter registers recoding letters and MSS sent by Haultain
Notebooks with translations from the classics
Portfolio of early studies in English history on: Athelstan; Becket; Henry I, II, and III; Simon de Montfors; Edward I, II and III; William the Conqueror, and more
Rough draft and proofs of a projected history of England up to the Restoration
Scrapbook of clippings, including reviews of "Canada and the Canadian Question"
Scrapbook of clipped reviews of "The United States", including some letters of criticism
Scrapbook of clippings on Canadian events, including reviews and comments on "The Bystander"
Scrapbook of Smith contributions to "The Week"
Scrapbook of clippings, mostly Smith letters to newspapers
"From a Trans-Atlantic Point of View", Smith article against Home Rule
Clippings preserved by G. Mercer Adam, but not containing the signature of Smith
Chapters of a book on the United Kingdom
Manuscript of "The Present and Future of Religion", a projected volume of contributions to the "New York Sun"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including The Schism in the Anglo-Saxon Race, The Weak Point of the Elective System, Article on Froude's "Divorce of Catherine of Aragon", a response to a toast at a Psi Upsilon meeting, and a lecture on The Old University through the Ages

did.unittitle: Notes and copies of exam questions used by Trinity College, and proof sheets of exams prepared by Smith at Trinity
Selections from "The Bystander", chosen by Haultain with the intention of publishing them
Scrapbooks of clippings by or about Smith
Bound group of clippings withdrawn from Cornell Library shelves
"Bystander" articles from the original files of the "Toronto Weekly Sun"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presented to Cornell by Walter Dymond Gregory, 1939

did.unittitle: 14 MS notebooks with Smith's translations of poetry, classical school work, and political and historical fragments
Notebook with printed pages, MS notes and typed copies of correspondence regarding Smith and St. George's
Press clippings, mostly reviews of Smith's published works

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Party Government", "The Future of Poetry", In the Court of History", "Nationality and Empire", "White Man's Burden", and the first four chapters of a school history of the US

did.unittitle: Volume II of Haultain's republication plan, entitled "Historical and Political America"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists of Oxford students, ethics, Greek, and Latin composition, Essay on murders of Caesar and Marat, address books and pocket diaries

did.unittitle: Waterman Hewett notebooks, mostly Smith's bibliographic notes and Hewett's travel itinerary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes file cards of references to Smith in works of other writers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks on England, list of Cornell students and notes to Howell, Bibliographic notes on religious parties

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Bases of the Empire", "The Founder of Christendom", "Progress or Revolution", "Labour and Socialism", "Unionism is not Socialism", "Socialism in Action"

did.unittitle: Literary articles assembled by Haultain for republication
Journal of Smith's mother Elizabeth Breton Smith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes records of births, deaths, family events, journeys to Paris, English Lakes, and Welshpool

did.unittitle: Lectures on English history, outlines, and fragments on Athenaeum Club stationary
Bills and Receipts, notes accompanying payment for articles, proofs and printed articles and letters to editors
Articles, pamphlets, and autograph address at cornerstone laying of Sage College
Copies of Smith's editorials in the "Weekly Sun"
Two scrapbooks of "Bystander" columns, plus pages of other scrapbooks
Smith pamphlets and articles clipped from periodicals, written by Smith, and many other clipped articles
Scrapbooks of clippings, Hewett's bibliography of Smith's writings, draft of "The United Kingdom"
Project for reprint publication by Haultain

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Grange, Smith, marble bust of Smith's wife, cartoons from Oxford of Smith

did.unittitle: Clippings, Smith's will copy, list of Smith's works at Art Gallery in Toronto, and photocopy of letter and medallion of Napoleon
